title: Interleaved FAIL/EMPTY goal escapes BOTH breakers: consecutive-FAIL breaker resets on an EMPTY cycle, goal-stall breaker resets on a FAIL — a goal alternating FAIL,EMPTY,FAIL,EMPTY lands nothing forever, caught by neither. Add a combined consecutive-NON-SHIPPING breaker.
summary: Surfaced in the 2026-07-18 go-review of goal-stall-escalation. The loop now has two independent non-progress breakers, each keyed to ONE outcome class: consecutiveFailBreaker (cmd_loop_control.go) counts consecutive core.VerdictFAIL and HALTS the batch at maxConsecutiveFails; goalStallTracker (cmd_loop_goalstall.go) counts consecutive EMPTY/BLOCKED (SkippedUnknown/SkippedAuditAdvisory) and self-files+continues at the threshold. Each RESETS on the other's outcome. So a goal that alternates FAIL, EMPTY, FAIL, EMPTY, … resets BOTH counters every cycle and crosses NEITHER threshold — it burns pipelines indefinitely while landing nothing, which is exactly the class both breakers exist to stop.
root_cause: The two breakers partition the non-shipping outcomes (FAIL vs EMPTY/BLOCKED) into disjoint counters instead of tracking the union. 'Landed nothing this cycle' is the real signal (FAIL, EMPTY, and BLOCKED all ship nothing); splitting it across two consecutive-counters means an interleaving of the two classes never accumulates in either.
fix: Add a THIRD counter (or generalize one) that counts consecutive NON-SHIPPING cycles regardless of class — i.e. any FinalVerdict that is not PASS and not SHIPPED_VIA_BUILD. On its (config-driven) threshold, escalate the SAME way goal-stall does (self-file a weighted inbox todo naming the goal + the mix of block/fail reasons, emit an abnormal-event) — never_stop_queue_inject_inbox, so escalate-and-continue, do not blanket-halt (the consecutive-FAIL breaker already owns the hard-halt decision for pure-FAIL streaks). Reuse the goalStallTracker shape (reasons already collected). Config: a `goal_stall.nonprogress_threshold` (compiled default e.g. 5, higher than the pure-EMPTY threshold since a mix is noisier). Regression test: FAIL,EMPTY,FAIL,EMPTY,FAIL with threshold 5 escalates on the 5th; a single PASS anywhere in the run resets it.
acceptance: A goal alternating FAIL/EMPTY for `nonprogress_threshold` cycles escalates (self-files + abnormal-event), where today neither breaker fires.
A shipping cycle (PASS / SHIPPED_VIA_BUILD) resets the non-progress counter.
Threshold config-driven with a compiled default; does NOT hard-halt the batch (escalate + continue).
go test -race on cmd/evolve PASS; no regression to the two existing breakers.
files: go/cmd/evolve/cmd_loop_goalstall.go (generalize the tracker or add a sibling)
go/cmd/evolve/cmd_loop.go (wire beside the existing FAIL + goal-stall checks)
notes: The fleet (Count>1) path also does not run these sequential-loop breakers at all (the wave/pool path continues before RunCycle) — a separate, pre-existing coverage gap the same review flagged; track under fleet-loop-breaker-parity if it recurs. Diagnostic-only escalation, never a blanket halt.
